title: 'Mossad or CIA': Vance hints at Epstein link to Israeli intel; admits 'screwed up' handling of files
content: JD Vance admitted that the Trump administration mishandled the communication surrounding the release of the Jeffrey Epstein files, blaming much of the confusion on former Attorney General Pam Bondi's public remarks. He also defended Donald Trump against allegations of trying to suppress the documents and suggested that intelligence agencies like Mossad or CIA had connections to Epstein.

title: Centre puts Delhi hospitals on alert as Sonam Wangchuk fast enters third week
content: Central government hospitals will now conduct twice-daily health checks for hunger strikers. Dr Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital is designated as the nodal facility for any emergencies. Advanced life support ambulances will remain stationed at the protest site. Medical teams will submit daily health status reports to the ministry. This action follows Delhi Police's request due to health concerns.

title: 1 dead, several vehicles crushed after crane collapses from high-rise in Mumbai
content: A 21-year-old man died and another was seriously injured after a large tower crane collapsed. The incident occurred at an under-construction building in Mira Road East on Thursday night. Several cars and two-wheelers parked inside the housing complex were also damaged. The crane fell partly within the construction site and into the society's parking area. An investigation into the exact cause of the crane collapse is currently underway.

title: Jeff Bezos donates $10m to LA that city's mayor says is about investing in culture
content: Jeff Bezos' Earth Fund has granted $10.1 million for Watts Towers Arts Center Campus improvements. This investment will expand usable park space and introduce new cooling features. The project aims to strengthen the campus as a cultural hub before the 2028 Olympics. Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass welcomed the significant milestone for the community. This funding advances commitments to green space and climate resilience efforts.

title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran; state TV says Bandar Abbas hit by projectiles
content: United States forces conducted a new wave of strikes against Iran on Thursday. These attacks marked the fifth consecutive night of military operations by the US. The strikes targeted Iran's military capabilities and facilities in Bandar Abbas. This escalation follows recent attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Relations between Washington and Tehran have sharply deteriorated amid these confrontations.

title: How Russian oligarch's superyacht used 'vanishing tech' to cross Hormuz
content: The superyacht Nord arrived in Bali after a mysterious journey.  It navigated the dangerous Strait of Hormuz by disabling its tracking system.  This massive vessel covered over four thousand nautical miles across the Indian Ocean.  The yacht, valued at $500 million, is equipped with advanced features.  Its operational costs are estimated at fifty million dollars annually.

title: Britain bought lifeless farmland for £13.8M, now beavers help it earn
content: A farm in Lincolnshire is evolving into a lucrative wildland endeavor, with beavers playing a pivotal role in this ecological project. The initiative focuses on demonstrating that restoring nature can lead to substantial private income. Current revenue streams are generated through carbon removal and biodiversity credits, with aspirations to draw significant investments for sustainable environmental solutions.

title: Canada to stop accepting new Parents and Grandparents Program applications
content: Canada is pausing new applications for the Parents and Grandparents Program. Existing applications will be processed, and 15,000 people will be approved in 2026. Family reunification remains a priority, and the super visa offers extended visits for relatives. The government aims to reduce processing times and improve predictability for families. Canada also warns citizens about potential marriage fraud in immigration sponsorship cases.

title: Does marital rape law shield men from unnatural sex prosecution?
content: The Supreme Court will examine if husbands can face charges for sexual acts causing harm. This question arises despite existing exemptions for marital rape under penal laws. The court will begin final hearings on these pending pleas from September ninth. The central issue is whether the court can create a new offense by striking down exceptions. The Centre opposes criminalizing marital rape, citing marriage as a unique relationship.

title: The Rangers could still trade Braden Schneider despite his new contract
content: The New York Rangers may have signed defenseman Braden Schneider to a new contract.  However, the team remains open to listening to trade offers for the young player.  This decision stems from the team's desire to acquire a new forward for the roster.  New York's defensive depth also contributes to Schneider surfacing in trade discussions.  The Rangers are exploring all options as they reshape their team for the upcoming season.

title: Why the Maple Leafs may rethink Auston Matthews' $53M future
content: Toronto Maple Leafs captain Auston Matthews is once again in the spotlight as fresh NHL trade rumors have emerged following the team’s offseason moves. Matthews has not asked for a trade, so there is no indication he is itching to leave. But it has been speculated that the club’s performance during the 2026-27 NHL season could go a long way toward impacting talks about his long-term future ahead of a pivotal contract time frame.

title: Taylor Swift faces backlash after keeping her wedding dress private
content: Taylor Swift faced serious online criticism regarding her wedding dress privacy choices. A cybercitizen called her decision performative and attention-seeking. Swift's presence at her husband Travis Kelce's teammate's wedding also drew scrutiny and intense commentary. Her gown reportedly overshadowed the bride's dress in a group photo. Some users defended Swift fiercely, attributing the criticism to seeking engagement.

title: Here's when the Fortnite v41.20 update goes live in your region
content: Fortnite's major v41.20 update arrives on July 16, 2026, bringing exciting new content.  The highly anticipated release includes a DC Comics Hot Bat Summer collaboration with popular characters.  Servers will undergo maintenance for approximately two hours, starting at 4 AM ET.  Players can expect new skins, items, and map changes across various game modes.  This update promises a significant refresh for the popular online gaming experience.

title: Formulate policy to free elderly, terminally ill prisoners: Supreme Court
content: The Supreme Court ordered states and Union Territories to create a policy for releasing elderly and terminally ill prisoners.  This directive ensures constitutional values are upheld even within prison walls.  The court emphasized that dignity and humane treatment remain paramount for all inmates.  Data shows over 5,000 prisoners fall into these vulnerable categories.  The policy must be formulated within the next three months.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for July 17, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for July 17, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: 'No sensitive data leaked': Govt refutes reports of Kudankulam nuclear plant breach
content: Union Minister Jitendra Singh dismissed reports of sensitive Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant files being leaked. He asserted that no nuclear safety or security data had been compromised in the incident. The Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited clarified leaked documents related only to the plant's conventional Balance of Plant package. This clarification followed claims by a ransomware group about accessing thousands of project-related files.

title: Man held for Farage shooting threat used British Asian's photo, Hindu posts
content: A man was arrested for threatening to shoot Nigel Farage in the head. He posted the threat on X, which was identified by security services. The suspect described himself as a terrorist and a Liberal Democrat online. Police arrested the man at a south London address after obtaining user information. He has since been released on police bail pending further inquiries.

title: '500 farmers join': Wangchuk gets support from Tikait, Kejriwal at 'packed' Jantar Mantar
content: Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike entered its nineteenth day with significant health concerns. Farmer leader Rakesh Tikait joined the protest, strengthening the ongoing agitation. Opposition leaders and parties offered their support, urging the minister's removal. Congress leaders appealed to Wangchuk to end his fast while assuring continued demands. Doctors warned of serious health complications if the hunger strike continues.

title: Woman waiting at Mangaluru bus stop hacked to death; one-sided love angle emerges
content: A tragic incident occurred when a 21-year-old woman was brutally attacked near the BC Road bus stand. Identified as Lavanya, she succumbed to her injuries and was pronounced dead at Bantwal Government Hospital. Investigators have identified Chetan, a distant relative with unresolved affections, as the alleged perpetrator, and efforts to apprehend him are ongoing.

title: Channi vs Warring in Punjab: Why Congress may not give in to pressure politics
content: Congress leadership struggles to unite Punjab leaders amid ongoing internal disputes. New leadership structure has triggered open conflict and fresh rounds of infighting. Former Chief Minister Charanjit Singh Channi is now leading a revolt against the current party president. The party leadership appears firm this time, avoiding past mistakes of succumbing to pressure. Managing these divisions is critical for Congress to regain political ground in Punjab.

title: 'Pahandi' procession, choked Grand Road : What triggered crowd scare at Puri Rath Yatra
content: During Puri's Rath Yatra, a sudden surge in the crowd resulted in a tragic death of one devotee. More than one hundred individuals were treated for suffocation, prompting emergency responders to evacuate some on stretchers near the Singhadwara. This event unfolded as thousands attended the Pahandi ritual and chariot procession, reigniting important discussions about crowd safety following last year's similar incident.

title: Harry Kane's 'Messi' remark fuels fresh 2030 World Cup speculation
content: England captain Harry Kane deferred decisions about his future World Cup participation.  Argentina secured a dramatic 2-1 semifinal victory over England on Wednesday.  The Three Lions will now face France in the third-place playoff match.  Argentina advanced to the final against Spain after their comeback win.  Kane stated he will evaluate his international future season by season.

title: Vivek Oberoi reacts to Sonam Wangchuk’s hunger strike: ‘I’m an actor, not a politician’
content: Vivek Oberoi has reacted to Sonam Wangchuk's ongoing hunger strike at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, saying he prefers to stay away from political debates but believes that people should have the freedom to express their views in a healthy democracy. While he did not comment on the specific demands or the issues surrounding the protest, the actor said he sees himself as someone who observes and learns from what is happening around him.

title: Kohli equals Viv Richards with another ODI half-century against England
content: Virat Kohli scored another ODI fifty against England in the second ODI at Cardiff, equalling Viv Richards with his 14th 50-plus score against the opposition. Only Kumar Sangakkara (15) has more. Kohli anchored India's innings as they reached 168/3 after 30 overs. India, leading the series 1-0, replaced the ill KL Rahul with Ishan Kishan.

title: The 49ers could land a $96M defensive star in a blockbuster trade rumor
content: Maxx Crosby's potential trade from the Las Vegas Raiders to the San Francisco 49ers is being discussed intensely by analysts. He is reportedly unhappy with the Raiders' lack of immediate Super Bowl contention. The 49ers need edge rush help while Nick Bosa and Mykel Williams recover from ACL injuries. Crosby's value as a trade chip is considered super high by analysts. A move could happen as the 49ers aim for immediate success.

title: 'Stop spreading baseless stories': Siya Goyal's father after spice shop sealed
content: Praveen Goyal claims his family faces unnecessary harassment after the FDA suspended their shop. He stated he is cooperating fully with the murder investigation into Ketan Agarwal's death. Goyal emphasized his daughter should receive the strictest punishment if found guilty. The FDA cited food safety violations and suspected adulteration during its inspection. A police team also visited Rajasthan as part of the ongoing murder probe.

title: Sonakshi Sinha, Fatima Sana Shaikh and Imran Khan back Sonam Wangchuk’s hunger strike
content: As Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike reaches its nineteenth day, prominent film industry figures rally behind him. Sonakshi Sinha and Fatima Sana Shaikh are leading a call for authorities to engage in constructive dialogue. Imran Khan has also raised his voice, demanding accountability in light of the NEET-UG controversy. Many more voices from the film world have echoed similar sentiments, advocating for discussions.

title: Virat Kohli breaks Ricky Ponting's record, enters all-time top-five elite list
content: Virat Kohli achieved a significant career milestone during the second ODI against England. He surpassed Ricky Ponting to become the fifth-most capped player in international cricket. Kohli scored 65 runs, contributing to India's strong position in the match. This half-century also drew him level with Viv Richards for fifty-plus scores against England. His remarkable longevity continues to be a defining aspect of his career.

title: Indralok Aawasiy Yojana 2 BHK flats: Eligibility, price, registration fee and lottery rules
content: Uttarakhand residents can now own homes through the Indralok Aawasiy Yojana in Haridwar.  This scheme offers two categories of flats for different income groups.  Registration requires a ten percent deposit and a nominal processing fee.  Applications are verified before a transparent lottery selects beneficiaries for these homes.  Successful applicants can pay in full or use an instalment plan.

title: 'Make Wangchuk education minister': Kejriwal joins protest, seeks Pradhan's ouster
content: Arvind Kejriwal joined Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike at Jantar Mantar. He demanded the removal of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Kejriwal also endorsed Wangchuk as a potential replacement for the minister. He highlighted recurring exam paper leaks and student suicides. The former Delhi CM urged the government to listen to youth concerns.

title: Rajasthan school teacher 'strip-searches' students over missing Rs 500, suspended
content: A teacher in Rajasthan was suspended following serious complaints from villagers. Locals alleged students were strip-searched after money went missing from a classroom. However, officials investigated and found these allegations to be entirely false. Only student bags were checked by other students, not the teacher herself. The department suspended the teacher to protect its image despite the false claims.

title: Cyberattack hits Japan's ice cream giant Glico, disrupts sushi & supermarket deliveries
content: A cyberattack on Nichirei has disrupted ice cream and fast food supplies across Japan. Popular companies like Ezaki Glico and KFC Japan are experiencing significant product delays. Some restaurants may face temporary menu changes and potential store closures due to shortages. This incident follows other recent cyberattacks targeting major Japanese corporations. The government urges companies to update systems and strengthen cybersecurity measures.

title: NEET-UG 2026 results: 11.21 lakh candidates qualify in re-test; women outshine men
content: Two candidates, Aryan Gupta from Punjab and Panshul Bansal from Haryana, secured the highest score of 715 (out of 720 marks) in the re-examination; 19 candidates crossed the 700-mark threshold; of the top 138 candidates (scores above 690), 93% cracked the exam on their first attempt, with 99% of them aged between 17 and 19 years

title: Monsoon season power crisis: Power curbs likely to continue in Kerala
content: Electricity Minister Sunny Joseph said that the State is currently facing a 500 MW to 600 MW deficit in availability

title: As Wangchuk’s health worsens, Delhi High Court orders regular monitoring
content: Wangchuk has lost over 9 kg after starting hunger strike and his body is consuming muscles and his organs may be next, says doctor; govt. assures High Court of necessary medical intervention

title: CB-CID begins probe into registration of Palani math land
content: A team of 15 police officers, led by Deputy Superintendent of Police Ajay Thangam, conducted inquiries in Palani.

title: Oil marketing companies yet to issue new LPG connections to domestic consumers
content: Many say they have been waiting since March, when the conflict began in West Asia. They complain that cooking with induction stoves is not always convenient for them

title: Kochi Corporation orders probe into spending of ₹6.32 crore for streetlight maintenance in 2022-23
content: Report on expenditure towards streetlight repair and maintenance tabled in council; survey to be conducted to verify number of LED lights that CSML claimed to have installed under City LED Street Light project last year and assess number of old lights that remain to be replaced, says Mayor

title: From ice stupas to hunger strikes: The journey of Sonam Wangchuk
content: For many Indians, Sonam Wangchuk is the real-life inspiration behind the character of Phunsukh Wangdu in 3 Idiots. But his story goes far beyond cinema. From transforming education in Ladakh and developing the world-famous Ice Stupa project to becoming the most prominent face of Ladakh’s movement for constitutional safeguards, Wangchuk’s journey has spanned education, climate action and activism. This explainer traces how an engineer and educator became one of India’s most influential public voices on Ladakh’s future.

title: 86 dams in Tamil Nadu, including Mettur and Vaigai, to come under the scanner of independent panels of experts
content: The first comprehensive dam safety assessment for each dam should be carried out on or before December 30, 2026

title: NIA to charge 10 ‘for role in J&K narco-terror syndicate’
content: They are accused of financing terrorist activities in J&K using money generated from narcotics smuggling

title: TV journalist quizzed by police in case relating to alleged attempt to bribe TVK MLA
content: Investigators found he had allegedly been in contact with the prime accused during the purported conspiracy

title: 12 COVID cases, 4 deaths reported in A.P. since June 26, but no need to worry, says health official
content: There has been no cluster outbreak, says the Health Secretary; describing the situation as periodic cyclical viral circulations, expert says that the infections may blow over by August end

title: DMK urges Centre to constitute tribunal on Mekedatu
content: A resolution to this effect was adopted at a virtual meeting of the MPs, attended by party president M.K. Stalin from London

title: In AI era, IT sector employees facing an uncertain future
content: The recent sacking of over 1,000 employees of two information technology firms in Kerala has triggered a disquiet in the sector. G. Ragesh talks to some of the affected employees and other stakeholders to find out what is ailing the sector

title: Minister highlights A.P.’s tourism potential at FAITH conclave in Delhi
content: Describing Andhra Pradesh as a complete tourism destination, Kandula Durgesh invites investors to partner with the State

title: A.P. Food Commission finds lapses at anganwadi centres and welfare hostels
content: Several institutions served notices over misuse of stocks, serving stale food to children and use of expired provisions in preparing mid-day meals at schools

title: Thousands participate in Jagannath Rath Yatra in Vijayawada
content: Beginning from Sitara Grounds, Rath Yatra culminates Gundicha Mandir near Seethamma Vaari Padalu; streets abuzz with chants of ‘Jai Jagannath’

title: Backward Classes leaders in A.P. demand fair deal
content: National BC Welfare Association demands basic amenities at welfare hostels,  34% reservations in local self-government institutions and an Act to prevent discrimination and social humiliation

title: Meeting on Cabinet expansion deferred as Mallikarjun Kharge is tied up with family emergency
content: The much-awaited discussion on the Cabinet expansion by Congress leaders in Delhi remained inconclusive owing to Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge being unavailable for meetings. The list for Cabinet expansion could be finalised only by next week

title: Will discuss if government amends Delimitation Bill as per Opposition’s demands, says Sanjay Raut
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader tells reporters in Nagpur that if the government brings up a different proposal, all the parties of INDIA bloc will sit together and come up with a collective decision

title: Leave with all your belongings, Mamata tells deserters
content: Mamata Banerjee, who is facing one of the biggest challenges of her political life, said those who remain with her will be her “greatest strength”, and she will rebuild the Trinamool Congress with them

title: IAF to participate in Exercise Pitch Black 2026 in Australia from July 20 to August 7
content: Hosted by Royal Australian Air Force, the exercise will involve up to 100 fighter and support aircraft and more than 2,500 military personnel operating from Darwin, Tindal and Amberley bases

title: Voices from Opposition, film industry rally in Wangchuk’s support
content: The Congress party urges the activist to end his fast, saying it has been fighting for the same issue; actors Sonakshi Sinha and Imran Khan voice their support for the movement

title: ‘There must be room for humanity’: J&K CM expresses concern over Wangchuk’s health
content: Omar Abdullah recalled Dr. Manmohan Singh-led government’s engagement with protesting activist Anna Hazare and his associates during a hunger strike in New Delhi

title: Roads to frontline as crucial as weapons in modern warfare: Rajnath Singh
content: At a conclave, Defence Minister says the BRO has evolved from a road construction agency into a leading strategic infrastructure organisation, as he cited landmark projects like the Atal Tunnel, Umling La Pass road and the Sela Tunnel

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: US Launches 5th Consecutive Night Of Strikes Against Iran
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.

title: 'SAARC, BIMSTEC Don't Need To Be Rivals': Bangladesh At Delhi Meet
content: According to a statement by the Ministry of External Affairs, the meeting pulled together security advisers and heads of delegations from all six other member states, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka and Thailand.

title: Weather Highlights: Orange Alert Issued For Heavy Rainfall In Parts Of Himachal
content: Weather Highlights: The IMD forecast rain or thundershowers at a few places in Uttar Pradesh, while warning of heavy rain, accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning, at isolated places in the eastern part of the state.

title: Trump Administration Tightens Visa Limits For Foreign Students And Journalists
content: Under a change that could be implemented as soon as September, foreigners on student visas would be admitted for the length of their academic programme up to a maximum of four years.

title: 28-Year-Old Shot Dead By Friend In Gurugram: Cops
content: A man allegedly shot his 28-year-old friend using his father's licensed revolver in Sector 65, took him to a hospital and fled after the victim was declared dead, police said on Thursday.

title: Man Pulls Out Machete From Bag, Kills Woman Waiting For Bus In Karnataka
content: Preliminary information suggests the accused pulled out a machete from his bag, following which the woman tried to flee. The accused chased her, attacked her with the weapon, and fled the scene.

title: Man Stabbed To Death After Argument Over Borrowed Money In Mumbai: Cops
content: A 24-year-old man was allegedly stabbed to death in Mumbai's Shivajinagar area as a result of a long-standing family dispute and an altercation over repayment of borrowed money, police said on Thursday.

title: Trump To Accuse China Of Meddling In US Elections In Primetime Speech: Report
content: Several of the country's top national security officials are expected to attend the speech. Those invited include the heads of the CIA, FBI, Office of the Director of National Intelligence, Department of Homeland Security and other senior officials.

title: Shiv Seva Spokesperson Shaina NC's WhatsApp Hacked
content: Shaina NC told NDTV that her WhatsApp has been hacked, and she has lodged a complaint with the cyber police.

title: Panic In Mira Road As Crane Falls From Under-Construction Tower
content: A 20-year-old man was killed and three others were injured after a crane collapsed from the 13th floor of an under-construction building in Thane district on Thursday, a police official said.

title: "Project Meghalaya" And Alleged Attempt To Destabilise Vijay Government
content: The case centres on claims that attempts were made to persuade at least one TVK MLA to defy the party whip during assembly voting.

title: No Supreme Court Live Stream In Hearings For Those Without Legal Counsel
content: Under the new policy, litigants appearing as party-in-person - those representing themselves without legal counsel - will generally be encouraged to participate in court proceedings through virtual hearings

title: EU Report Raises Rights Concerns As Pakistan Reaps Billions In Trade Benefits
content: Pakistan pocketed roughly "732 million in EU tariff exemptions last year, but a new joint assessment from the European Commission and the EU's foreign policy arm paints a troubling picture of the human rights record.

title: CCTV Emerges After Gurugram Thar Driver Runs Over Domestic Worker
content: The victim was identified as Guddi, 57, a resident of Mahoba in Uttar Pradesh. Police earlier said she worked at an apartment in the same society.

title: 1 Dead, Several Taken To Hospital After Crowd Surge At Puri Rath Yatra
content: One person died and several others were injured after a crowd surge at the iconic Rath Yatra celebrations in Odisha's Puri on Thursday.

title: Troops In Hormuz Islands, Bombing Pickaxe Mountain: Trump's Big Iran War Plan
content: Trump held a high-level meeting in the White House Situation Room on Tuesday night, where senior officials discussed several military options.

title: 1 Dead, 3 Injured As Crane Collapses From 13th Floor Near Mumbai
content: Several vehicles parked below the under-construction high-rise suffered extensive damage from the impact

title: Ram Temple Theft Report Ready, To Be Submitted Anytime Now: Sources
content: The three-member SIT was formed by the UP government on June 13 at the request of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust

title: 12-Year-Old Girl Bludgeoned To Death At Home. Sexual Assault Suspected
content: A stone recovered at the scene is believed to have been used to bludgeon her to death. Wardha Superintendent of Police Saurav Kumar Agrawal said that investigators suspect sexual assault took place before the murder.

title: NTA declares NEET UG 2026 results; over 11.21 lakh qualify
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: Brains without borders: US central bank picks merit over MAGA hysteria
content: five task forces are charged with rethinking the central bank's approach to monetary policy . they propose "transformational changes" to prepare the institution for a rapidly changing economy . former central bankers, academics and corporate executives tapped to lead the effort .

title: 2 dead, 78 in hospital after rath yatra crowd surge in Puri
content: Debabrata Mohapatra is an Assistant Editor at The Times of India, Bhubaneswar. He had been writing for TOI from Puri since 2006 before joining the Bhubaneswar bureau in August 2010. He covers crime, law & order and Congress. Read More
